CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INT, date_col DATE);
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, date_col) VALUES (1, current_date());
SELECT id, date_col FROM test_dates WHERE date_col = current_date();
CREATE TABLE another_table (t_id INT, related_date DATE);
INSERT INTO another_table (t_id, related_date) VALUES (1, current_date() - INTERVAL '5 days');
SELECT a.id, a.date_col FROM test_dates a JOIN another_table b ON a.id = b.t_id WHERE b.related_date < current_date();
SELECT id, date_col, (current_date() - date_col) AS days_difference FROM test_dates WHERE date_col IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_dates WHERE date_col = current_date() OR date_col < current_date() - INTERVAL '1 year';
SELECT id, date_col FROM test_dates WHERE date_col BETWEEN current_date() - INTERVAL '30 days' AND current_date();
SELECT id FROM test_dates WHERE date_col = (SELECT MAX(date_col) FROM test_dates WHERE date_col <= current_date());
SELECT a.id FROM test_dates a WHERE a.date_col = current_date() AND a.id IN (SELECT t_id FROM another_table WHERE related_date = current_date() - INTERVAL '2 days');
